item.title: The Private Credit Industry Is Growing Up. That’s Rarely a Painless Process
item.description: Also: Who’s actually buying Cybertrucks?

item.title: Emerging Currencies Erase War Losses as Traders Bet on Peace
item.description: A key gauge of emerging-market currencies has fully recovered its losses from the start of the US-Iran war, driven by optimism that the conflict is de-escalating after Iran reopened the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: US Eyes More Namibian Uranium Imports as AI Drives Nuclear Push
item.description: The US is weighing boosting uranium imports from Namibia, the world’s third-biggest producer, and supporting greater output of the nuclear fuel through its mining companies and government financing, America’s top diplomat in the southern African nation said.

item.title: Canada Stocks Recover Losses From Iran Conflict as Strait Opens
item.description: Canadian stocks jumped Friday and reversed all of their losses since the war in the Middle East began after Iran said the Strait of Hormuz is open to commercial ships, fueling hopes the conflict may be nearing an end.

item.title: Bitcoin Climbs to Two-Month High Amid Middle East Deal Optimism
item.description: Bitcoin climbed to the highest level since early February after a flurry of comments from the US and Iran sparked optimism that the conflict in the Middle East may be heading toward a resolution.

item.title: Venezuela Bonds Rally as IMF Resumes Formal Contact with Caracas
item.description: Venezuela’s dollar bonds are rallying on Friday as the International Monetary Fund’s decision to resume contact with authorities in Caracas lifts investor sentiment toward the nation’s defaulted debt.

item.title: Apple Upgraded as BNP Paribas Sees Path to Increase Market Share
item.description: Apple Inc. was upgraded to outperform from neutral by BNP Paribas, which said the iPhone maker can use the recent spike in memory prices as a way to increase its share of smartphone market share.

item.title: Iran Conflict Poses Risk to Global Economy, IMF Members to Say
item.description: The conflict in the Middle East is a major shock that poses a “serious threat to the global economy” and will hit the poorest countries the hardest, according to a statement expected to be released by the Chair of the International Monetary and Financial Committee Friday.

item.title: Russia’s Main Black Sea Port Resumes Loading Crude at Key Berth
item.description: Russia’s largest Black Sea port of Novorossiysk resumed loading crude from a key berth, according to people familiar with the information.

item.title: Treasuries Rally as Oil Tumbles on Mideast Peace Deal Optimism
item.description: Treasuries jumped, sending yields to their lowest levels in a month, as oil prices tumbled on optimism a Middle East peace deal is within reach.

item.title: Intel Shares Soar to Highest Since 2000 on Turnaround Optimism
item.description: Intel Corp. shares leaped to their highest intraday level since the dot-com era on Friday as optimism that the chipmaker’s turnaround plan is working continues to grow.

item.title: France and Greece to Renew Defense Pact and Broaden Cooperation
item.description: France and Greece are set to renew their defense pact next week as the European nations seek to further shore up their deterrence strategy.

item.title: Empty LNG Tankers Attempt Hormuz Crossing Into Persian Gulf
item.description: Two liquefied natural gas tankers appear to be attempting to enter the Persian Gulf via the Strait of Hormuz, which — if they succeed — would be a first during the Middle East energy crisis.

item.title: Aluminum Drops After Iran Says Strait of Hormuz Is Fully Open
item.description: Aluminum prices sank in London after Iran said the Strait of Hormuz was open for cargoes to transit, a move that would allow the metal to be exported out of key producers Bahrain, Saudi Arabia and the United Arab Emirates.

item.title: Stocks Rally as Oil Plunges on Reopening of Hormuz Strait
item.description: US stocks rallied Friday after Iran said it would open the Strait of Hormuz following the truce between Israel and Lebanon, promising to ease an oil shock that has shadowed the global economic outlook ever since President Donald Trump started the war seven weeks ago.

item.title: US Natural Gas Futures Waver on Plunging Oil Price, Lower Output
item.description: US natural gas futures fluctuated between slight gains and losses, paring an earlier rise as Iran’s foreign minister said the Strait of Hormuz was “completely open.” The news sent oil prices plunging, triggering immediate financial outflows from baskets of energy products that include the US gas contract. The tandem price move happened despite near-term supply and demand for US gas remaining fundamentally unaffected by such geopolitical developments.

item.title: BOE’s Pill Suggests Rates May Need to Rise to Tackle Inflation
item.description: Bank of England Chief Economist Huw Pill said that interest rates may need to rise to contain the threat of inflation, even as energy prices tumbled on Friday.

item.title: Brokers Flock to Paradise of Sun, Sand and ‘Unlimited’ Leverage
item.description: Offshore havens like the Seychelles are enabling online trading firms to offer high-risk bets to retail investors.

item.title: Lumina Metals, Shareholder Seek $251 Million in Toronto IPO
item.description: Lumina Metals Corp. and one of its shareholders are seeking to raise C$343.7 million ($251 million) in an initial public offering in Toronto, building on a modest recovery in Canadian market debuts.

item.title: Dollar Wipes Out Iran War Gain as Tehran Says Hormuz Is Open
item.description: The dollar wiped out all the gains it registered since the US-Iran war began after Tehran announced that the Strait of Hormuz is now “completely open” for commercial traffic, further denting demand for haven assets.

item.title: ECB’s Lagarde Says Risks to Inflation Outlook Tilted to Upside
item.description: European Central Bank President Christine Lagarde said dangers to the region’s price outlook are skewed to the upside as officials continue to assess the economic damage being wrought by the Iran conflict.

item.title: Trump Says US Blockade Remains After Iran Opens Hormuz
item.description: Ships at the Khor Fakkan Container Terminal in the Sharjah Emirate, along the Strait of Hormuz. Photographer: Giuseppe Cacace/AFP/Getty Images

item.title: Nvidia Helps Make Quantum Computing CEO a Billionaire in Days
item.description: The founder of quantum computing startup Xanadu Quantum Technologies Ltd. became a billionaire in a matter of days after Nvidia Corp. threw its weight behind the emerging technology.

item.title: Adani’s North Mumbai Project to Cost $11 Billion, Official Says
item.description: The Adani Group could invest as much as $11 billion to build homes and offices in North Mumbai, as the ports-to-power conglomerate expands its real estate footprint in India’s financial capital.

item.title: BAE Market Value Seen Topping $100 Billion on Defense Spending
item.description: Analysts expect BAE Systems Plc’s market value to top $100 billion for the first time as the UK defense contractor benefits from increased military spending by the US and Gulf states in the aftermath of the Iran war.

item.title: EQT Warns of Exit Risks for Alternative Energy Assets Held by PE
item.description: EQT AB, Europe’s biggest private equity firm, says the path to exiting investments in clean-energy developers and operators faces a growing number of hurdles.

item.title: From Cars to Cans: How the Iran War Is Affecting World’s Aluminum Supply
item.description: Aluminum is a ubiquitous metal that’s favored by manufacturers for its abundance and low cost. But the industry is built on a complex and sometimes fragile supply chain.

item.title: Kast’s Campaign to Balance Chile Budget Gets Off to Tough Start
item.description: Chile’s President José Antonio Kast went on TV this week and warned the nation it couldn’t keep racking up debts like it was — then outlined a series of tax cuts and subsidies that may widen the deficit in the near term.

item.title: European Power Prices Set to Close Week Below Pre-War Levels
item.description: European power futures are trading below where they were before the Middle East war began, amid a surge in renewables generation coupled with easing gas prices driven by optimism over a ceasefire.

item.title: US Premarket Movers: Alcoa, Ally Financial, Netflix, NiSource
item.description: S&P 500 Index futures rise 0.3% as of 7:45 a.m. in New York as traders add more risk after the market’s rally.
